channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Dow loses more than 500 points Friday as tech slumps and yields spike: Live updates
item.description: Stocks fell on Friday, bogged down by losses in technology stocks and a rise in U.S. Treasury yields.

item.title: Trump went big on tech stocks in first quarter of 2026, new filings show
item.description: Trump bought shares of Amazon, Meta, Oracle, Broadcom, Motorola and Dell worth millions, new ethics disclosure filings show.

item.title: What you need to know about Nvidia competitor Cerebras after wild IPO
item.description: Nvidia competitor Cerebras made a stunning debut on Wall Street Thursday, signaling unstoppable demand for AI chips. Here's how its chips compete with Nvidia's.

item.title: Berkshire Hathaway returns to airlines with $2.6 billion stake in Delta Air Lines
item.description: The Omaha-based company built a position worth more than $2.6 billion, making Delta Berkshire's 14th-largest holding at the end of March.

item.title: Sundheim's D1 Capital bought several tech stocks last quarter — with one big exception
item.description: D1 Capital bought into a plethora of well-known technology stocks in the first quarter, with the exception of a social media giant.

item.title: Trump touted Palantir on Truth Social after buying the company's stock, records show
item.description: During the first three months of the year, Trump purchased between $630,000 and $247,008 worth of Palantir stock and sold at least $1.1 million.

item.title: Traders now see next Fed interest rate move as a hike following inflation surge
item.description: The fed funds futures market is pricing in an increase as soon as December.

item.title: Democrats blast Trump over 'slush fund' in possible IRS lawsuit settlement
item.description: ABC News reported President Donald Trump could settle a lawsuit against the IRS in exchange for a $1.7 billion fund to compensate his allies.

item.title: FAA cuts target for air traffic control staffing
item.description: The FAA said Friday it was reducing its target for air traffic control staffing, vowing to modernize scheduling and increase the time employees spend managing traffic.

item.title: Stephen Miran exits the Fed. How he set the stage for Kevin Warsh.
item.description: The outgoing Fed governor shares big ideas with the incoming chair.

item.title: Maine might boot Sen. Susan Collins. It could hurt the state's wallet for years
item.description: Sen. Susan Collins is the lone New England Republican left in Congress and chairs the Senate Appropriations Committee.

item.title: Warren Buffett, Stephen Curry lunch auction fetches $9 million for charity
item.description: Buffett, Curry and his wife Ayesha Curry will join the winner and their guests on June 24 in Omaha, Nebraska, where Berkshire Hathaway is based.

item.title: Trump told Xi ‘I don't talk about’ whether U.S. would defend Taiwan from China
item.description: President Donald Trump spoke about Taiwan after a two-day visit to China, where he said he discussed Iran, and trade deals with Chinese President Xi Jingping.

item.title: Wall Street and Main Street face off next week with Nvidia, consumer earnings. Here's what's ahead
item.description: Stocks have been ripping higher thanks to a revival in enthusiasm around artificial intelligence, but without much follow through in other parts of the market.

item.title: How China and U.S. eased the Middle East oil shock and kept prices from spiking even higher
item.description: China and the U.S., the world's two largest economies, wield great influence over the oil market and are using it to help plug the supply gap.

item.title: Israel and Lebanon agree to extend ceasefire by 45 days, U.S. State Dept says
item.description: Israel and Lebanon have agreed to a 45-day extension of a ceasefire U.S. President Donald Trump declared on April 16, the U.S. State Department said on Friday.

item.title: AI chip bubble rivals French stocks in 1700s, surpasses Nasdaq during dot-com frenzy by one measure
item.description: Historical parallels for the artificial intelligence bubble are in no short supply.

item.title: David Tepper’s Appaloosa nearly doubles Amazon stake, adds Sandisk in the first quarter
item.description: Amazon became Appaloosa's largest disclosed equity holding at the end of March, a regulatory filing showed.

item.title: Chinese EVs are coming to Canada, and some dealers can't wait to sell them
item.description: Canada is allowing 49,000 Chinese-made electric vehicles to be imported for retail sales annually at a tariff rate of 6.1%.

item.title: NFL wants certain trading contracts banned from prediction markets like 'first play of game,' injuries
item.description: The letter, which was reviewed by CNBC, calls for banning certain contracts and raising the age requirements for participation on sports-related contracts.

item.title: Can the S&P 500 hold on for a 7th weekly gain? Plus, Friday's best and worst stocks
item.description: Every weekday, the Investing Club releases the Homestretch; an actionable afternoon update just in time for the last hour of trading.

item.title: How to plan a trip for medical tourism and the best resources
item.description: If you're seeking a medical procedure abroad, these resources can help.

item.title: Inflation rate projected to hit 6% in the second quarter, top economic forecasters say
item.description: The recent surge in inflation is likely to get worse over the next several months, according to a survey Friday.

item.title: Starbucks to lay off 300 U.S. employees, shutter some regional support offices
item.description: Starbucks said the moves will help the coffee chain return to profitable growth.

item.title: Exxon's once-hefty dividend is now tiny. Here's how to fix that
Bristol Myers Squibb turns to China to develop new drugs in newest cross-continent collaboration
item.description: U.S. drugmaker Bristol Myers Squibb inked a partnership with a Chinese company that some say could mark the next wave of collaboration across continents.

item.title: Trump-Xi summit: The 3 big takeaways from historic meeting in Beijing
item.description: The two-day meeting wrapped up Friday, setting the tone for further U.S.-China talks this year.

item.title: Detroit automakers have cut more than 20,000 U.S. salaried jobs as AI threat looms
item.description: Reasons for the job declines vary by automaker, but are generally tied to evolving technological changes in the industry — including the rise of AI.

item.title: 'Biggest bottleneck in the AI buildup' fuels DRAM ETF to record
item.description: The Roundhill Memory ETF (DRAM) just reached $10 billion in assets at the fastest pace ever for an exchange-traded fund according to TMX VettaFi.

item.title: China will buy more U.S. oil because it is a natural trade partner, says Energy Secretary Wright
item.description: China relies heavily on crude oil imports from the Middle East but those supplies are mostly cut off due to Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.
